title: Understanding Impulse
text: Impulse is the change in momentum of an object when a force is applied over a period of time. It can be calculated using the formula: Impulse $$=$$ Change in Momentum.

title: Calculate Initial Momentum of the Bullet
text: The initial momentum of the bullet can be calculated using the formula: Momentum $$=$$ mass * velocity. What is the initial momentum of the bullet?

title: Initial Momentum of the Bullet
text: Calculate the initial momentum of the bullet using its mass and velocity.

title: Calculate Final Momentum of the Bullet and Block
text: After the collision, the bullet and block move together. Calculate their combined momentum using the formula: Momentum $$=$$ (mass of bullet + mass of block) * velocity after collision.

title: Final Momentum of the Bullet and Block
text: Calculate the final momentum of the bullet and block using their combined mass and velocity after collision.

title: Impulse Calculation
text: The impulse on the bullet is equal to the change in momentum of the bullet. Use the formula: Impulse $$=$$ Final Momentum - Initial Momentum.

title: Calculate Impulse
text: Calculate the impulse on the bullet using the change in momentum.

title: Direction of Impulse
text: The direction of the impulse is the same as the direction of the force applied. Since the bullet is moving east, the impulse is also directed east.
